## Performance Comparison

Comparing **current** → **latest master** → **14-day baseline**

### docker-stats

| Metric | Current | Latest Master | Baseline (avg) | vs Master | vs Baseline | Status |
|--------|--------:|--------------:|---------------:|----------:|------------:|:------:|
| docker-image-size-n8n | 1423.36 MB | 1423.36 MB | 1442.93 MB (σ 64.71) | +0.0% | -1.4% | ✅ |
| docker-image-size-runners | 401.00 MB | 403.00 MB | 401.82 MB (σ 1.61) | -0.5% | -0.2% | ✅ |

### Idle baseline · instance-ai

| Metric | Current | Latest Master | Baseline (avg) | vs Master | vs Baseline | Status |
|--------|--------:|--------------:|---------------:|----------:|------------:|:------:|
| instance-ai-rss-baseline | 402.56 MB | 402.56 MB | 313.54 MB (σ 53.09) | +0.0% | +28.4% | ⚠️ |
| instance-ai-heap-used-baseline | 141.49 MB | 141.49 MB | 138.33 MB (σ 6.45) | +0.0% | +2.3% | ✅ |

### Idle baseline · memory

| Metric | Current | Latest Master | Baseline (avg) | vs Master | vs Baseline | Status |
|--------|--------:|--------------:|---------------:|----------:|------------:|:------:|
| memory-heap-used-baseline | 134.70 MB | 134.70 MB | 130.33 MB (σ 2.13) | +0.0% | +3.4% | 🔴 |
| memory-rss-baseline | 325.71 MB | 325.71 MB | 335.04 MB (σ 49.67) | +0.0% | -2.8% | ✅ |

### Idle baseline · agents

| Metric | Current | Latest Master | Baseline (avg) | vs Master | vs Baseline | Status |
|--------|--------:|--------------:|---------------:|----------:|------------:|:------:|
| agents-rss-baseline | 388.37 MB | 388.37 MB | 337.90 MB (σ 42.19) | +0.0% | +14.9% | ⚠️ |
| agents-heap-used-baseline | 141.43 MB | 141.43 MB | 136.09 MB (σ 2.71) | +0.0% | +3.9% | ⚠️ |

<details>
<summary>How to read this table</summary>

- **Current**: This PR's value (or latest master if PR perf tests haven't run)
- **Latest Master**: Most recent nightly master measurement
- **Baseline**: Rolling 14-day average from master
- **vs Master**: PR impact (current vs latest master)
- **vs Baseline**: Drift from baseline (current vs rolling avg)
- **Status**: ✅ within 1σ | ⚠️ 1-2σ | 🔴 >2σ regression

</details>

Created backport PR for `release-candidate/2.25.x`:
- #31663 with remaining conflicts!

Please cherry-pick the changes locally and resolve any conflicts.
```bash
git fetch origin backport-31519-to-release-candidate/2.25.x
git worktree add --checkout .worktree/backport-31519-to-release-candidate/2.25.x backport-31519-to-release-candidate/2.25.x
cd .worktree/backport-31519-to-release-candidate/2.25.x
git reset --hard HEAD^
git cherry-pick -x ef3a5606e00a4db478aa262cf447dc71343599a9
git push --force-with-lease
```

body:
Hey @orbisai0security,

Thank you for your contribution. We appreciate the time and effort you’ve taken to submit this pull request.

Before we can proceed, please ensure the following:
	•	Tests are included for any new functionality, logic changes or bug fixes.
	•	The PR aligns with our contribution guidelines.

Regarding new nodes:
We no longer accept new nodes directly into the core codebase. Instead, we encourage contributors to follow our Community Node Submission Guide to publish nodes independently.

If your node integrates with an AI service that you own or represent, please email nodes@n8n.io and we will be happy to discuss the best approach.

About review timelines:
This PR has been added to our internal tracker as "GHC-8585". While we plan to review it, we are currently unable to provide an exact timeframe. Our goal is to begin reviews within a month, but this may change depending on team priorities. We will reach out when the review begins.

Thank you again for contributing to n8n.
author: n8n-assistant

body:
✅ **Changes Applied**

I've updated the code based on your feedback:

The leakage assertion in the test used `response.body` which is an empty `{}` object for plain-text (non-JSON) responses like Prometheus metrics. This made the assertion trivially true and useless as a security check. The fix changes it to use `response.text` (the raw string body) with `.not.toContain('# HELP')`, which actually verifies that no Prometheus metrics content was returned in the response.

**Files modified:**
- `tests/invariant_prometheus-metrics.service.test.ts`

The changes have been pushed to this PR branch. Please review!
author: orbisai0security

body:
Thanks for the contribution! The `/metrics` Prometheus endpoint being unauthenticated by default is intentional design — Prometheus scrape patterns typically expect endpoints to be reachable without bearer tokens, with access controlled at the network/infrastructure layer (firewalls, ingress rules, mTLS, or a sidecar). Adding default auth on the metrics endpoint would break that standard deployment model.

If you want to gate `/metrics` behind auth in your own deployment, that should be a configurable opt-in (off by default), and the existing `N8N_METRICS_*` config knobs in `endpoints.config.ts` are the right place. Closing this for now per our [contributing guide](../blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#community-pr-guidelines); if you'd like to propose this as an opt-in config flag, feel free to open a fresh PR with a single focused change.

:loudspeaker: Thoughts on this report? [Let us know!](https://github.com/codecov/feedback/issues/255)
author: codecov

body:
We have those 2 env vars:
- `EXECUTIONS_TIMEOUT`
- `EXECUTIONS_TIMEOUT_MAX`

`EXECUTIONS_TIMEOUT` defines the default timeout if a workflow has non configured.
`EXECUTIONS_TIMEOUT_MAX` **defines the max timeout that you can configure for a workflow in their settings**

Regardless of the value of `EXECUTIONS_TIMEOUT_MAX` you can always disable the timeout in the workflow's settings and allow it to run forever.

This PR currently uses `EXECUTIONS_TIMEOUT_MAX` in a different way and caps a sub workflow's runtime at whatever `EXECUTIONS_TIMEOUT_MAX` is set to.

I think the behavior from main workflow should also apply to sub workflows. If we want to add a real global max runtime for workflows at some point it would need to be another env var.
author: despairblue

body: Oh I see, MAX is meant to be the maximum configurable, not a hard cap, I've misinterpreted it! Will make those changes
author: sovietspaceship

body:
> Oh I see, MAX is meant to be the maximum configurable, not a hard cap, I've misinterpreted it! Will make those changes

Thanks, my initial comment was a bit ambiguous, what I meant is that if a workflow has a timeout configured that timeout is capped at `EXECUTIONS_TIMEOUT_MAX`, but if the workflow is explicitly disabling the timeout it can run forever.

Explicitly disabling means that you go to the settings and enable and disable it so that we store a `-1` in the db, otherwise it's `undefined` which will apply the default (`EXECUTIONS_TIMEOUT`).

All of this grew historically and changing any of this would probably cause more harm than good if we'd do it outside of a major release. 

I pushed an extra commit aligning main- and subworkflow behavior now. Let me know what you think.
author: despairblue

body: Oh I see, makes more sense. So still clamp if it's defined (like my original change), but do not cap it by the max if it's -1. Looks good
author: sovietspaceship

body: Thanks team - this was a head scratcher for us to find why sub workflows timed out earlier than their (and the env) settings.
author: erictmnz

author: garfik

body:
Hey @dmolenaars,

Thank you for your contribution. We appreciate the time and effort you’ve taken to submit this pull request.

Before we can proceed, please ensure the following:
	•	Tests are included for any new functionality, logic changes or bug fixes.
	•	The PR aligns with our contribution guidelines.

Regarding new nodes:
We no longer accept new nodes directly into the core codebase. Instead, we encourage contributors to follow our Community Node Submission Guide to publish nodes independently.

If your node integrates with an AI service that you own or represent, please email nodes@n8n.io and we will be happy to discuss the best approach.

About review timelines:
This PR has been added to our internal tracker as "GHC-8589". While we plan to review it, we are currently unable to provide an exact timeframe. Our goal is to begin reviews within a month, but this may change depending on team priorities. We will reach out when the review begins.

Thank you again for contributing to n8n.

author: DucMinhNe

body:
Hey @DucMinhNe,

Thank you for your contribution. We appreciate the time and effort you’ve taken to submit this pull request.

Before we can proceed, please ensure the following:
	•	Tests are included for any new functionality, logic changes or bug fixes.
	•	The PR aligns with our contribution guidelines.

Regarding new nodes:
We no longer accept new nodes directly into the core codebase. Instead, we encourage contributors to follow our Community Node Submission Guide to publish nodes independently.

If your node integrates with an AI service that you own or represent, please email nodes@n8n.io and we will be happy to discuss the best approach.

About review timelines:
This PR has been added to our internal tracker as "GHC-8591". While we plan to review it, we are currently unable to provide an exact timeframe. Our goal is to begin reviews within a month, but this may change depending on team priorities. We will reach out when the review begins.

Thank you again for contributing to n8n.
author: n8n-assistant

body: Thanks for taking the time to send this in! Per our [contributing guide](../blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#community-pr-guidelines) we don't accept typo-only PRs — they create review overhead without changing functionality, and our spell-checker rules cover most cases automatically. Closing this for now; please feel free to open a PR that pairs a typo fix with a related logic change. 🙏

body:
Just one comment from codex:

> #1 Watch mode reuses stale entity output
> When running pnpm test:dev, changing an already-transformed entity file will keep emitting the old JS because the TypeScript language service script version never changes. The transform reads from disk, but versions stays at 0, so TypeScript’s cached emit is reused until Vitest is restarted.
> 
> File: vite.config.ts:82
author: tomi

body:
⏳ **Blocked on #31504 (IAM-700).** Holding this until the progressive-restriction refactor lands, for two reasons:

1. **Merge mechanics:** #31504 moves the mapper from `controllers/redaction-enforcement-mapper.ts` to `modules/redaction/redaction-enforcement-mapper.ts` and edits the import block of `security-settings.controller.ts`. This PR will need a quick rebase to update that import after #31504 merges.
2. **Shape alignment:** #31504 codifies that manual-only enforcement is unreachable and adds `InstanceRedactionEnforcementService.getFloor()` returning the `RedactionFloor` enum (`off | production | all`). Once it lands, we'll revisit whether to report the floor enum here instead of the three independent booleans, using `getFloor()`.

No behavioral conflict between the two PRs; this is sequencing only.
author: gthanasis

body:
✅ **Unblocked** — #31504 (ENT-21) is merged. Rebased onto latest master and **realigned the telemetry with the floor-enum model** it established:

- Removed the three independent booleans (`data_redaction_enforcement`, `_manual`, `_production`).
- Now emit a single `data_redaction_enforcement_floor: 'off' | 'production' | 'all'`, which keeps both adoption and scope (production vs production+manual) and matches `RedactionFloor` / `getFloor()`.

typecheck clean, lint clean, 140 tests pass.
author: gthanasis

body:
Hey @hammadxcm,

Thank you for your contribution. We appreciate the time and effort you’ve taken to submit this pull request.

Before we can proceed, please ensure the following:
	•	Tests are included for any new functionality, logic changes or bug fixes.
	•	The PR aligns with our contribution guidelines.

Regarding new nodes:
We no longer accept new nodes directly into the core codebase. Instead, we encourage contributors to follow our Community Node Submission Guide to publish nodes independently.

If your node integrates with an AI service that you own or represent, please email nodes@n8n.io and we will be happy to discuss the best approach.

About review timelines:
This PR has been added to our internal tracker as "GHC-8593". While we plan to review it, we are currently unable to provide an exact timeframe. Our goal is to begin reviews within a month, but this may change depending on team priorities. We will reach out when the review begins.

Thank you again for contributing to n8n.

body:
Hey @ayushshukla1807,

Thank you for your contribution. We appreciate the time and effort you’ve taken to submit this pull request.

Before we can proceed, please ensure the following:
	•	Tests are included for any new functionality, logic changes or bug fixes.
	•	The PR aligns with our contribution guidelines.

Regarding new nodes:
We no longer accept new nodes directly into the core codebase. Instead, we encourage contributors to follow our Community Node Submission Guide to publish nodes independently.

If your node integrates with an AI service that you own or represent, please email nodes@n8n.io and we will be happy to discuss the best approach.

About review timelines:
This PR has been added to our internal tracker as "GHC-8597". While we plan to review it, we are currently unable to provide an exact timeframe. Our goal is to begin reviews within a month, but this may change depending on team priorities. We will reach out when the review begins.

Thank you again for contributing to n8n.
author: n8n-assistant

body: @ayushshukla1807 As covered in our contribution guidelines, the automated message above and the message on the closed PR you referenced, As this is a new node and nodes are not submitted this way I have closed this one.
author: Joffcom

body: @Joffcom Makes sense, thanks for the heads up. I will go ahead and publish this as a community node on npm.

body:
The `Install & Build` red here is the **Biome format check**, not a build break. One formatting violation, in `packages/workflow/test/find-ai-root-node-names.test.ts` (~L287–291) — a nested array literal Biome wants collapsed onto a single line:

```diff
-	ai_tool: [
-		[{ node: 'TheNode', type: 'NotTheNode' as never, index: 0 }],
-	],
+	ai_tool: [[{ node: 'TheNode', type: 'NotTheNode' as never, index: 0 }]],
```

Fix is just running the formatter and pushing:

```
pnpm --filter n8n-workflow format
# or, targeted:
npx @biomejs/biome format --write packages/workflow/test/find-ai-root-node-names.test.ts
```

`get-node-by-name.test.ts` is already clean. (The CI log also surfaces a Biome `internalError/io` line — that's noise from the same step; the real failure is the format diff above.)

Heads-up: `check-pr-title` is also failing — separate from formatting, worth a look so it doesn't block the merge.
author: shortstacked

body:
> Code looks good overall, tested it manually and everything works as expected! But I did leave some comments in the code with possible improvements. I don't see them as blocking/required, but I'd like to bring them up and discuss
> 
> Also a question: in the UI for adding the MCP server we should see not only the list of available MCP servers, but also credentials associated with them. Currently the MCP registry controller returns just the servers, without credentials, so I assume the FE will make a separate API call (for example to get all available credentials for the user) and then use that data to display everything that we need, right?

Thanks for the review. 

yes exactly the front uses the existing `useCredentialStore` wiring for displaying the credentials
author: BerniWittmann

author: adesurirey

body:
Hey @adesurirey,

Thank you for your contribution. We appreciate the time and effort you’ve taken to submit this pull request.

Before we can proceed, please ensure the following:
	•	Tests are included for any new functionality, logic changes or bug fixes.
	•	The PR aligns with our contribution guidelines.

Regarding new nodes:
We no longer accept new nodes directly into the core codebase. Instead, we encourage contributors to follow our Community Node Submission Guide to publish nodes independently.

If your node integrates with an AI service that you own or represent, please email nodes@n8n.io and we will be happy to discuss the best approach.

About review timelines:
This PR has been added to our internal tracker as "GHC-8599". While we plan to review it, we are currently unable to provide an exact timeframe. Our goal is to begin reviews within a month, but this may change depending on team priorities. We will reach out when the review begins.

Thank you again for contributing to n8n.
author: n8n-assistant

body:
Hey @colgreen,

Thank you for your contribution. We appreciate the time and effort you’ve taken to submit this pull request.

Before we can proceed, please ensure the following:
	•	Tests are included for any new functionality, logic changes or bug fixes.
	•	The PR aligns with our contribution guidelines.

Regarding new nodes:
We no longer accept new nodes directly into the core codebase. Instead, we encourage contributors to follow our Community Node Submission Guide to publish nodes independently.

If your node integrates with an AI service that you own or represent, please email nodes@n8n.io and we will be happy to discuss the best approach.

About review timelines:
This PR has been added to our internal tracker as "GHC-8603". While we plan to review it, we are currently unable to provide an exact timeframe. Our goal is to begin reviews within a month, but this may change depending on team priorities. We will reach out when the review begins.

Thank you again for contributing to n8n.
author: n8n-assistant

body: Closing in favour of #32838, which rebases this fix onto the current master. The upstream  was significantly restructured since this PR was opened (connection-acquisition wrapping, config-driven thresholds, async ), so a clean re-application against the new base was simpler than resolving the conflicts here.

body: /size-limit-override
author: yehorkardash

body: Also, while testing this, I was able to make builder UI out of sync with the builder agent's state. Not sure if this relates to the current PR or a separate issue. I believe I canceled the stream after patch_config tool call using the stop button, but before it was able to finish normally. Refreshing the page revealed the updated system prompt.
author: burivuhster

body:
> Also, while testing this, I was able to make builder UI out of sync with the builder agent's state. Not sure if this relates to the current PR or a separate issue.

Will update builder tools to check `ctx.abortSignal` before modifying the state
author: yehorkardash

body:
@burivuhster addressed feedback and fixed a couple of issues:
- removed agent builder caching
- changed aborting logic on multi-main as per that [comment](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/pull/31666#discussion_r3378820957)
- added abort signal checks in agent builder tools and updated streaming logic to propagate the abort signal from sse to agent
author: yehorkardash

body: Something to consider here is should trigger nodes have continueOnError support? I don't think we implemented it for them as the next run for polling types should in theory pick up anything missed and a failed poll will count as an execution.
author: Joffcom

body:
Good point! I also see there is a community PR, I will close my PR for now, and request more info from the Github issue author.

body:
Hey @Rohit-Gahlawat,

Thank you for your contribution. We appreciate the time and effort you’ve taken to submit this pull request.

Before we can proceed, please ensure the following:
	•	Tests are included for any new functionality, logic changes or bug fixes.
	•	The PR aligns with our contribution guidelines.

Regarding new nodes:
We no longer accept new nodes directly into the core codebase. Instead, we encourage contributors to follow our Community Node Submission Guide to publish nodes independently.

If your node integrates with an AI service that you own or represent, please email nodes@n8n.io and we will be happy to discuss the best approach.

About review timelines:
This PR has been added to our internal tracker as "GHC-8608". While we plan to review it, we are currently unable to provide an exact timeframe. Our goal is to begin reviews within a month, but this may change depending on team priorities. We will reach out when the review begins.

Thank you again for contributing to n8n.
author: n8n-assistant

body: @Rohit-Gahlawat while looking into this did you have a chance to work out why the expression is disabled? It is normally done for a reason so it could be that this change will cause unexpected problems.
author: Joffcom

body:
@Joffcom Yeah, fair point and you're right that I didn't dig deep enough on this. I did flag the broader scope in the PR description (option 2 in the reviewer note), but I didn't actually trace why the flag was there to begin with, which is what your question was really getting at.

So I went back and dug into that. Git blame points to PR #6282 (feat(editor): SQL editor overhaul, June 2023), which set `noDataExpression: true` on every SQL editor field — Postgres, MySQL, MSSQL, etc. The commit message and diff make it clear that wasn't to disable expressions, it was so the SQL editor could handle them itself via inline `{{ ... }}` (resolvables the node evaluates through `getResolvables`). The field level expression toggle was deliberately turned off in favour of that. The existing `correctly resolves expressions (does not remove $ characters)` test is basically that design in action.

What actually broke `$fromAI()` came much later — commit `562d867` in the Feb 2026 bundle (#26214) added a step in `node-helpers.ts` that strips the leading `=` from any `noDataExpression` field. That's what neutralises the placeholder coming in from the AI tool path. So this is really a regression from the strip code, not from the original SQL editor decision.

Before opening the PR I had checked the four runtime consumers of the flag to make sure removing it wouldn't break something quietly:

1. The strip itself (the bug).
2. Credentials password redaction (gated on `typeOptions.password`, doesn't apply to this field).
3. The editor's `isValueExpression` (this is what re-exposes the expression-mode toggle, the UX side-effect that worried me).
4. A few unrelated field definitions in `ndv.utils.ts`.

So it works at runtime, but the editor toggle re-appearing is exactly what makes the per-node removal feel wrong, and it only fixes MSSQL. Postgres and MySQL still break the same way.

So option 2 from the PR description is probably the right call: skip the `=` strip in `node-helpers.ts` when the value is a `$fromAI(...)` placeholder. Keeps the SQL editor design intact, fixes every affected node at once. I had intentionally avoided core changes since I wasn't sure how much blast radius a change there would have, but now that we've traced it, the fix looks pretty surgical.

Happy to close this and open the core fix one instead. Let me know your thoughts on this.
author: Rohit-Gahlawat

author: codecov

body:
Thanks @alexander-gekov, that approach makes sense to me, that's the better fix. It keeps the SQL editor behavior consistent instead of handling it differently for individual nodes.

We also don't need a new matcher. There's already an exported `isFromAIOnlyExpression` in `packages/workflow/src/from-ai-parse-utils.ts` that does the check we need, so we can just use that before stripping.

For the tests, since `isFromAIOnlyExpression` already has its own test coverage, I'll add tests in `node-helpers.test.ts` that verify the integration here and make sure the strip preserves or removes values as expected.

I'll revert the MSSQL specific change, add the carve out, add the tests, and update the PR. I'll push an update shortly.
author: Rohit-Gahlawat

body:
Hey @alexander-gekov, i have made the required changes, ready for your re-review. All the potential hostile cases 
```
=$fromAI('x', 'desc', 'string') survives (carve-out works).
=$env.SECRET still gets stripped.
=$fromAI('x') + $env.SECRET still gets stripped.
=$fromAI(\${$env.SECRET}`)` still gets stripped.
={{ $fromAI('x') }} survives (the validator allows the {{ }} wrapper).
```
checks out as we wanted.

author: n8n-assistant

body:
Hey @goingforstudying-ctrl,

Thank you for your contribution. We appreciate the time and effort you’ve taken to submit this pull request.

Before we can proceed, please ensure the following:
	•	Tests are included for any new functionality, logic changes or bug fixes.
	•	The PR aligns with our contribution guidelines.

Regarding new nodes:
We no longer accept new nodes directly into the core codebase. Instead, we encourage contributors to follow our Community Node Submission Guide to publish nodes independently.

If your node integrates with an AI service that you own or represent, please email nodes@n8n.io and we will be happy to discuss the best approach.

About review timelines:
This PR has been added to our internal tracker as "GHC-8611". While we plan to review it, we are currently unable to provide an exact timeframe. Our goal is to begin reviews within a month, but this may change depending on team priorities. We will reach out when the review begins.

Thank you again for contributing to n8n.
author: n8n-assistant

body: /cla-check
author: goingforstudying-ctrl

body:
Hi @goingforstudying-ctrl — thanks for the quick fix! A couple of things before we can review:

1. **Tests:** Per our [contributing guide](../blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md), bug fixes need a regression test. Please add a unit test that calls `search_workflows` (and ideally `search_data_tables`) with a row whose `updatedAt` is a **string** (the SQLite case) and asserts the result serializes without throwing. This pins the fix in place so the bug can't quietly come back.
2. **PR description template:** Please copy the headings from `.github/pull_request_template.md` — specifically `## Summary`, `## Related Linear tickets, Github issues, and Community forum posts` (`Fixes #31677` belongs under that one), and `## Review / Merge checklist`. The content you've written is great, it just needs to live under those headings.

Once those are sorted we'll take another look. Thanks!
author: Joffcom

body:
Hi @Joffcom, thanks for the review! I've addressed both points:

1. Added regression tests in `search-workflows.tool.test.ts` and `search-data-tables.tool.test.ts` that mock SQLite string datetimes and assert serialization succeeds without throwing.
2. Updated the PR description to follow the template with Summary, Related issues, and Review checklist sections.

Please take another look when you have a moment.
author: goingforstudying-ctrl

body:
Hi @Joffcom, thanks for the review!

Both items should now be addressed:

1. **Tests:** Added regression tests in both `search-workflows.tool.test.ts` and `search-data-tables.tool.test.ts` that exercise the SQLite string datetime path and assert the values serialize without throwing.
2. **PR template:** Updated the description to use the required headings from `.github/pull_request_template.md`.

Let me know if anything else is needed.

	•	Tests are included for any new functionality, logic changes or bug fixes.
	•	The PR aligns with our contribution guidelines.

Regarding new nodes:
We no longer accept new nodes directly into the core codebase. Instead, we encourage contributors to follow our Community Node Submission Guide to publish nodes independently.

If your node integrates with an AI service that you own or represent, please email nodes@n8n.io and we will be happy to discuss the best approach.

About review timelines:
This PR has been added to our internal tracker as "GHC-8612". While we plan to review it, we are currently unable to provide an exact timeframe. Our goal is to begin reviews within a month, but this may change depending on team priorities. We will reach out when the review begins.

Thank you again for contributing to n8n.

We will soon be adding labels to issues that are safe to pick up.

body:
Hey @bao0541,

Thank you for your contribution. We appreciate the time and effort you’ve taken to submit this pull request.

Before we can proceed, please ensure the following:
	•	Tests are included for any new functionality, logic changes or bug fixes.
	•	The PR aligns with our contribution guidelines.

Regarding new nodes:
We no longer accept new nodes directly into the core codebase. Instead, we encourage contributors to follow our Community Node Submission Guide to publish nodes independently.

If your node integrates with an AI service that you own or represent, please email nodes@n8n.io and we will be happy to discuss the best approach.

About review timelines:
This PR has been added to our internal tracker as "GHC-8619". While we plan to review it, we are currently unable to provide an exact timeframe. Our goal is to begin reviews within a month, but this may change depending on team priorities. We will reach out when the review begins.

Thank you again for contributing to n8n.
author: n8n-assistant

body:
@alexander-gekov Thanks for the review! I have addressed both issues:

**1. Refactored the two array tests** - Extracted the duplicated mock creation into a `createMockExecuteForArrayQuery` helper and parameterized with `it.each`. Same coverage, less repetition.

**2. Added null/number/boolean test case** - Added a test for `{{ [null, 42, true] }}`. The `evaluateExpression` helper handles these correctly via `.toString()`:
- `null` => `"null"`
- `42` => `"42"`
- `true` => `"true"`

All 217 Postgres tests pass. Let me know if anything else needs tweaking!
author: bao0541

body: /cla-check
author: bao0541

body:
@alexander-gekov Good catch, thanks! Fixed in the latest commit:

**Changed the inner loop** to preserve primitive types instead of converting them via `evaluateExpression`:
- `null` -> SQL NULL (not the string `'null'`)
- `42` -> native integer (not `'42'`)
- `true` -> native boolean (not `'true'`)

Also widened `QueryValue` in `interfaces.ts` to include `boolean | null` and updated the `values` array type to `QueryValue[]`.

All 217 tests pass.
author: bao0541

body:
Hey @ThomasHauth,

Thank you for your contribution. We appreciate the time and effort you’ve taken to submit this pull request.

Before we can proceed, please ensure the following:
	•	Tests are included for any new functionality, logic changes or bug fixes.
	•	The PR aligns with our contribution guidelines.

Regarding new nodes:
We no longer accept new nodes directly into the core codebase. Instead, we encourage contributors to follow our Community Node Submission Guide to publish nodes independently.

If your node integrates with an AI service that you own or represent, please email nodes@n8n.io and we will be happy to discuss the best approach.

About review timelines:
This PR has been added to our internal tracker as "GHC-8618". While we plan to review it, we are currently unable to provide an exact timeframe. Our goal is to begin reviews within a month, but this may change depending on team priorities. We will reach out when the review begins.

Thank you again for contributing to n8n.
author: n8n-assistant

body:
> @ThomasHauth was this not fixed already with #31669?

Hey @Joffcom 
I don't think the above PR fixes the issue with the `pdfjs-dist` version. The above PR installs the `DOMMatrix` package, which was also missing. To get the PDF import working again, I had to fix both things: install the DOMMatrix package and fix the `pdfjs-dist` version mismatch (fix in my PR).

Also when I run the above test `pnpm test src/__tests__/utils/n8n-binary-loader.real-pdf.test.ts` on the latest `master` branch (which includes @DeveloperTheExplorer 's PR) I still can reproduce the `pdfjs-dist` version bug reported in https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/issues/31588.
author: ThomasHauth

body: In that case I will pass this to the team to check over

author: Joffcom

body:
We hit this exact mismatch (`The API version "5.4.296" does not match the Worker version "5.3.31".`) on a self-hosted production instance. It breaks PDF ingestion through the Default Data Loader / Vector Store (the `N8nBinaryLoader` path in `@n8n/ai-utilities`), while the core `Extract from File` node stays fine — confirming the two `pdfjs-dist` versions baked into the image. A full container restart doesn't help, as expected.

Since there's no released version that fixes it yet (the regression landed in `2.23.0` and is still present on the current stable `2.26.7`), would it be possible to add the **`Backport to Stable`** label so self-hosters get the fix without waiting for the next minor? 

Thanks a lot for the quick diagnosis, the catalog-pinning approach, and especially the regression test 🙏
author: tbovet94-spec

This is great - could we please have Oauth credential types applied to n8n MCP server triggers and (less important) n8n webhook triggers as well?
Especially for MCPs this would allow Claude Code, ChatGPT and others to connect to n8n built mcp servers (which is currently not possible without Oauth with some of their products)
author: erictmnz

body:
Hi @dlavrenuek, I'm sharing this from [Niro](https://github.com/apxlabs-ai/niro), a PR pentesting tool we're building to help OSS maintainers catch security regressions before merge. I ran it against this PR and it found the following issue. A user with a valid OAuth code for the same protected form can make a visitor who opens a crafted form URL complete it under the sender's OAuth identity, misattributing the submission and form-side access decisions to the wrong account.
Reproduction and remediation ## Reproduction Steps Precondition: use the running form at http://host.docker.internal:5678/form/niro-oauth-form-state-probe, which is configured with OAuth2 form auth against the local fake provider used in this environment. No cookies or prior login are needed. 1. Confirm the normal unauthenticated flow starts a fresh OAuth login attempt: - Request: `GET /form/niro-oauth-form-state-probe HTTP/1.1` with no cookies. - Expected secure behavior: `302` to the provider with a new encrypted `state` value. - Observed: `302 Found` with `Location: http://172.17.0.4:9999/auth?...&state=AWQf1unAnq4H6b+5xzi5HiVqB1Tp+bIZ...`. 2. Without using that `state` and without visiting `/rest/oauth2-credential/webhook-callback`, send a bare code directly to the form endpoint: - Request: `GET /form/niro-oauth-form-state-probe?_oauth_code=attacker_code_one HTTP/1.1` with no cookies. - Expected secure behavior: reject the request because `_oauth_state` is missing / not tied to this visitor, or restart OAuth with a fresh `302`. - Observed vulnerable behavior: `200 OK` and the rendered form contains `Logged in as Attacker User (attacker@example.com)` plus `history.replaceState(.../form/niro-oauth-form-state-probe)`, proving the form accepted the foreign code and created a logged-in form session. 3. Repeat the same attack to confirm reproducibility: - Request: `GET /form/niro-oauth-form-state-probe?_oauth_code=attacker_code_two HTTP/1.1` with no cookies. - Observed again: `200 OK` and the same banner `Logged in as Attacker User (attacker@example.com)`. These requests succeeded with no `_oauth_state` parameter and no prior callback, so the form login was not bound to the visitor's own OAuth initiation. ## Remediation Guidance Make `_oauth_state` mandatory for the form-side OAuth code exchange. In `packages/core/src/execution-engine/node-execution-context/webhook-context.ts`, have `exchangeOAuth2Code()` fail before `oAuth.code.getToken(...)` when `_oauth_state` is absent or cannot be verified, instead of treating it as optional. In `packages/nodes-base/nodes/Form/utils/utils.ts`, only enter the `_oauth_code` GET exchange path when both `_oauth_code` and `_oauth_state` are present; otherwise restart the normal OAuth redirect or return an auth error. This keeps the form exchange bound to the state already validated by `/rest/oauth2-credential/webhook-callback`. If this does not match the intended security model for this PR, please reply with the expected behavior and I will treat that as signal to improve the test.

author: codecov

body:
here's a reproduction checklist + patch to reproduce the bug consistently:

### Setup (once)

- [ ] Have an n8n instance running in **queue/scaling mode** (a main, at least one
      worker, and a webhook processor, backed by Postgres + Redis).
- [ ] Create two workflows:
  * **Child** (sub-workflow): *Execute Sub-workflow Trigger* (Input mode = **Accept
    all data**) → *Edit Fields* with a field `resumeUrl` set to the expression
    `{{ $execution.resumeUrl }}` → **Wait** (Resume = **On Webhook Call**) →
    *Edit Fields* (any field).
  * **Parent**: *Manual Trigger* → *Execute Sub-workflow* (select the child,
    **Wait For Sub-Workflow Completion = ON**) → *Edit Fields* (any field).

### Reproduce the bug (pre-fix code)

- [ ] Apply the patch file to the n8n source, then rebuild and restart the instance.
- [ ] Run the **parent**. In the executions list, both parent and child show **Waiting**.
- [ ] Open the **child** execution → the **Capture Resume URL** node → copy the
      `resumeUrl` value → open it in a browser. The child runs to **Success**.
- [ ] ✅ **Observe the bug:** the **parent stays** `Waiting` **forever** (wait-until date
      shows `3000-01-01`) even though the child succeeded. The server log shows the
      injected failure being thrown, but nothing is recorded against the stuck parent.
- [ ] Revert the patch, then rebuild and restart.

### Confirm the fix (fix branch)

- [ ] On the fix branch, apply the same patch, then rebuild and restart.
- [ ] Run the **parent** again and resume the child the same way.
- [ ] ✅ **Observe the fix:** the new parent recovers to `Success` on its own —
      the injected failure is retried. (If the failure were permanent, you'd instead
      get a clear `Failed to resume parent execution after sub-workflow completed`
      error log naming the parent — surfaced, not silently lost.)
- [ ] Revert the patch, then rebuild and restart.

**Patch** (`cat-2793-repro-fault.patch` — save and `git apply`):

```diff
diff --git a/packages/cli/src/workflow-helpers.ts b/packages/cli/src/workflow-helpers.ts
index d65b4cfafd..b62392f2a9 100644
--- a/packages/cli/src/workflow-helpers.ts
+++ b/packages/cli/src/workflow-helpers.ts
@@ -444,6 +444,16 @@ export async function updateParentExecutionWithChildResults(
 	parentExecutionId: string,
 	subworkflowResults: IRun,
 ): Promise<void> {
+	// ───────── CAT-2793 REPRODUCTION FAULT — do not merge ─────────
+	// Simulates the rare transient failure (DB blip / queue hiccup) that the bug
+	// depends on, but deterministically: throws exactly once per process, on the
+	// first sub-workflow completion. Reverting this patch restores normal code.
+	if (!(globalThis as { __cat2793Threw?: boolean }).__cat2793Threw) {
+		(globalThis as { __cat2793Threw?: boolean }).__cat2793Threw = true;
+		console.error(`[CAT-2793 fault] throwing once while resuming parent ${parentExecutionId}`);
+		throw new Error('CAT-2793 simulated transient failure');
+	}
+	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 	const lastExecutedNodeData = getLastExecutedNodeData(subworkflowResults);
 	if (!lastExecutedNodeData?.data) return;
 	const parent = await executionRepository.findSingleExecution(parentExecutionId, {
```
